message.content:
import { Map, Swords, Trophy, Target, Shield, Sparkles } from 'lucide-react'
import { Callout } from '@/components/mdx/Callout'
import { Card, CardGrid } from '@/components/mdx/CardGrid'
import { Checklist } from '@/components/mdx/Checklist'
import { FAQ } from '@/components/mdx/FAQ'
import { Steps, Step } from '@/components/mdx/Steps'
import { Section } from '@/components/mdx/Section'
import { YouTubeEmbed } from '@/components/mdx/YouTubeEmbed'

export const metadata = {
  title: "Anime Tactical Simulator Beginner Guide: Master the Basics 2026",
  description: "Learn how to progress fast in Anime Tactical Simulator with our comprehensive 2026 beginner guide. Master units, raids, towers, and gamepasses.",
  category: "Guide",
  image: "https://placehold.co/800x400/1e293b/fff?text=Anime+Tactical+Beginner+Guide",
  date: "2026-03-04",
  author: "Anime Tactical Wiki Team"
}

<Callout type="info" title="Quick Guide">
- **Primary Objective**: Follow the **anime tactical simulator beginner guide** to unlock World 2 as quickly as possible by farming Yen and completing tutorial quests.
- **Unit Management**: Never sell Mythic units early; instead, use the **Laboratory** to evolve them for massive damage boosts.
- **Gem Priority**: Focus your Gems on **Unit Equip** and **Card Opens** gamepasses before spending on luck or cosmetics.
- **Daily Routine**: Clear **Tower floors** daily to unlock additional unit slots without spending Robux.
- **Raid Strategy**: Only attempt Nightmare mode raids once you have at least three **Mythic-tier units** to ensure efficient clearing times.
</Callout>

Anime Tactical Simulator has quickly become one of the most engaging strategic experiences on Roblox in 2026. This game challenges players to build a formidable team of anime-inspired warriors to conquer various worlds, each filled with unique bosses and escalating difficulties. Navigating the complex systems of unit evolution, trait rerolling, and resource management can be overwhelming for newcomers. This **anime tactical simulator beginner guide** is designed to streamline your journey, ensuring you don't waste precious Gems or Yen on low-tier investments. By understanding the core loop of summoning, questing, and upgrading, you can transition from a novice to a top-tier player in record time. Whether you are aiming to dominate the leaderboards or simply want to enjoy the progression with friends, the following strategies will provide the essential foundation needed to succeed in the current meta.

<Section icon={Map} title="World Progression and Core Gameplay">

The journey begins on Planet Namek, the first world where you will learn the fundamental mechanics of attacking and questing. Unlike traditional tower defense games, your units in Anime Tactical Simulator follow a "click-to-attack" or "auto-attack" proximity system. Your primary goal is to maximize Damage Per Second (DPS) to clear waves of enemies that do not fight back, making efficiency the only real barrier to progression.

**Video Highlights:**
- **Fast Progression**: Learn the optimal path from Planet Namek to the latest endgame maps.
- **Gamepass Strategy**: Discover which passes are essential for free-to-play players using Gems.
- **Evolution Mechanics**: A breakdown of how to sell units for souls to power up your main team.

<YouTubeEmbed videoId="dZlDpBZ8P7E" title="Anime Tactical Simulator Beginner Guide (Fast Progression!)" />

To unlock new worlds, you must accumulate Yen by defeating enemies and completing NPC quests. Each world features a significant jump in enemy health, requiring you to upgrade your team before moving forward.

| World | Unlock Cost | Key Features | Primary Boss |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Planet Namek** | Free | Tutorial, Shenron Wishes | Frieza / Goku |
| **Marineford** | 100,000 Yen | Devil Fruits, Stat Laboratory | Luffy / Doflamingo |
| **Demon Forest** | 1,000,000 Yen | Awakening Dungeon, Talents | Akaza / Kokushibo |
| **Shadow Realm** | 10,000,000 Yen | Double Dungeons, Player Stats | Jin Woo / Igris |

<Callout type="success" title="Progression Tip">
Always prioritize finishing the main story quests in a world before farming bosses. The quest rewards provide the massive Yen injections needed to unlock the next map.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Swords} title="Unit Management: Keep, Fuse, or Evolve">

Your units are the heart of your tactical prowess. Units range from Common to Secret rarities, with Secret units being the rarest and most powerful. Managing your inventory effectively is the difference between a cluttered bag and a high-performance team.

<CardGrid cols={3}>
  <Card title="Keep">
    - **Mythic & Secret Units**
    - High DPS percentage units
    - Rare event-limited characters
  </Card>
  <Card title="Fuse">
    - **Common & Rare Units**
    - Duplicate Legendaries
    - Units used for XP fodder
  </Card>
  <Card title="Sell">
    - **Excess Legendaries**
    - Mythics (only for Souls)
    - Low-tier starter units
  </Card>
</CardGrid>

Evolution is a critical mechanic handled by the Lady Knight NPC in the lobby. By selling units of a specific rarity, you gain "Souls" of that rarity. These souls, combined with a duplicate of the unit you wish to evolve, allow you to increase their Star level.

| Star Level | Damage Bonus | Requirement |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **1 Star (Bronze)** | +25% Damage | 1 Duplicate + Souls |
| **2 Star (Silver)** | +75% Damage | 2 Duplicates + Souls |
| **3 Star (Gold)** | +100% Damage | 3 Duplicates + Souls |

<Callout type="tip" title="Evolution Strategy">
Do not waste souls on Legendary units. Save all your Mythic and Secret souls for your endgame DPS carries, as these resources are harder to accumulate in the early game.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Sparkles} title="Essential Gamepasses and Currency Management">

One of the most player-friendly aspects of Anime Tactical Simulator is that almost all gamepasses can be purchased using Gems earned in-game. This makes the **anime tactical simulator beginner guide** essential for free-to-play users who need to know where to spend their hard-earned currency first.

| Gamepass | Priority | Gem Cost | Benefit |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Unit Equip +2** | **Extreme** | 2,500 | Increases active team size |
| **Card Open +3** | **High** | 4,000 | Speeds up summoning process |
| **Card Access** | **High** | 5,000 | Summon while doing other tasks |
| **VIP** | **Medium** | 7,000 | +15% Gold and +5% XP gain |
| **Luck Boost** | **Low** | 15,000+ | Increases rare drop chances |

<Callout type="warning" title="Avoid Early Storage">
Do not spend Gems on inventory storage in the first few days. Use the Auto-Fuse and Auto-Sell features to keep your inventory clean and focus your Gems on power-increasing passes instead.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Target} title="Tower and Raid Strategies">

The Tower and Raid modes are where you will find the most valuable rewards outside of the main story progression. The Tower is a 100-floor challenge that tests your team's endurance and scaling.

<Steps>
  <Step num="1" title="Initial Tower Push">
    Reach Floor 10 as soon as possible to unlock your first additional unit slot.
  </Step>
  <Step num="2" title="Gem Farming">
    Every 5th floor provides a 500 Gem bonus. Use these to buy the Unit Equip gamepass.
  </Step>
  <Step num="3" title="Raid Preparation">
    Once you have a full team of Level 10 units, enter the Dragon Ball Raid to farm Legend Medals.
  </Step>
  <Step num="4" title="Accessory Grinding">
    Farm Nightmare Raids for Secret accessories like Jin Woo's Daggers for massive stat boosts.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Checklist
  id="daily-checklist"
  title="Daily Efficiency Checklist:"
  items={[
    "Complete all daily quests for Yen and Gems",
    "Use the Alucard Spin every 30 minutes for free potions",
    "Push at least 5 floors in the Tower",
    "Farm 10 Raids for Stat Cubes and Medals",
    "Check for new active codes in the settings menu"
  ]}
/>

<Callout type="info" title="Multiplayer Scaling">
Raids scale significantly with the number of players. If you are struggling with a boss's HP, try running the raid solo or with a highly coordinated duo rather than a full group of six.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Shield} title="Advanced Systems: Talents and Traits">

Once you reach the Demon Forest and Shadow Realm, basic leveling isn't enough. You must dive into the Talent and Trait systems to maximize your units' potential. Talents are upgraded using Stat Cubes found in raids, while Traits are rerolled using world-specific Medals.

| Medal Type | Source | Usage |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Legend Medal** | World 1 Raid | Reroll Dragon Ball units |
| **Pirate Medal** | World 2 Raid | Reroll One Piece units |
| **Hino Medal** | World 3 Raid | Reroll Demon Slayer units |
| **Shadow Medal** | World 4 Raid | Reroll Solo Leveling units |
| **Premium Medal** | Codes / Robux | Reroll any unit (High luck) |

<Callout type="tip" title="Range Upgrade">
Use your Movement Medals from achievements to upgrade your **Attack Range** first. This allows your units to hit multiple enemies simultaneously, drastically increasing your farming speed.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Trophy} title="Frequently Asked Questions">

<FAQ items={[
  {
    question: "How do I get more unit slots in Anime Tactical Simulator?",
    answer: "You can unlock extra slots by reaching floors 10, 25, 50, and 100 in the Tower mode, or by purchasing the Unit Equip gamepasses in the Gem store."
  },
  {
    question: "What is the best way to farm Gems as a beginner?",
    answer: "The fastest way to get Gems is by clearing the Tower for the first time (500 Gems every 5 floors) and completing the world-specific main quests which offer large Gem bundles."
  },
  {
    question: "Should I use my Premium Medals on Legendary units?",
    answer: "No. Premium Medals are rare and should be saved exclusively for Secret or top-tier Mythic units in the late game, as they offer the highest chance for S-tier traits."
  },
  {
    question: "Are there any active codes for Anime Tactical Simulator in 2026?",
    answer: "Yes, developers frequently release codes for Gems and Potions. Check the official Discord or the in-game 'Codes' UI section regularly for the latest updates."
  }
]} />

</Section>
